"'''The Rebel Four'''" is a 4-page comic story that appeared in ''[[Star Wars Tales 9]]'' and was collected in the TPB collection ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 3]]''. Both the script and art is provided by [[Jay Stephens]]. The story is a parody of the Fantastic Four comic series.

==Plot summary==
[[File:TheRebelFour.jpg|right|thumb|250px|The Rebel Four spring into action.]]
On the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vatleria]], long abandoned by its inhabitants, [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] patrol the [[stone ruins]], where they have established a new base. Unbeknownst to the [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]], they are being observed by an [[IG-series/Legends|IG-series]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]], who confirms the accuracy of information supplied by an [[Unidentified informant|informant]] regarding the Empire's presence on the world to their three shadowy companions. One of the members questions the droid on what their plan is, to which they respond that they will [[Mission to Vatleria|stop]] the Imperials. The droid brings their team, the [[Rebel Four]], into a huddle, sharing a confident belief that they will overcome their Imperial adversaries. Loudly announcing "It's Rebellin' time," the team runs into the ruins and their names are revealed: The droid is [[F4-MF]], and they are accompanied by the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Grimgrim]], the [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]]-wielding [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Soo Rcharrz]], and the [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]]-equipped humanoid [[Falaem Onn]].<ref name="Rebel Four" />

The team attacks the stormtroopers, observed through a [[Viewscreen/Legends|viewscreen]] by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], who dismisses the team's attack and presses a button. The Rebel Four are suddenly ensnared by [[Rope/Legends|ropes]] which pull each of them into their own unique [[Death/Legends|death]] chambers, where they are grusomely executed. F4-MF is stretched into pieces, Onn is [[Fire/Legends|burned]] to death, Grimgrim is crushed by rocks, and Rcharrz is obliterated. In the aftermath, Vader expresses disatisfaction at how easily the Rebels were defeated, and expresses a desire to face a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] to an [[Unidentified Imperial officer (Vatleria)|Imperial officer]]. The officer is incredulous and believes that Vader should be proud of the Empire's victory, and the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] responds by [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinetically strangles]] the officer. The ending text reveals that Vader will fulfill his desire for a [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|duel]] with a Jedi will soon be fulfilled, and that Vader will ultimately find peace.<ref name="Rebel Four" />

==Release and reception==
"The Rebel Four" is a four page [[Star Wars comic books|comic story]] written<ref name="Rebel Four" /> pencilled, inked, lettered, and colored by [[Jay Stephens]].<ref name="Epic Collection" /> The comic was created for the anthology series ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'', published by [[Dark Horse Comics]].<ref name="Rebel Four" /> "The Rebel Four" was first published in the [[Star Wars Tales 9|ninth issue]] of the series,<ref name="Rebel Four" /> which was originally intended to be published on [[September 12]], [[2001]],<ref name="September 2001">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20010607.html|text=September 2001 Comics}}</ref> but was eventually released on [[October 10]], 2001.<ref name="October 10">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/00-458/Star-Wars-Tales-9-photo-cover|text=Star Wars Tales 9}}</ref> The comic includes an introduction by assistant editor [[Philip Simon]] in which the various creative talents involved are represented as [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]], with Stephens working next to artist [[David McCaig]].<ref name="Rebel Four" />

"The Rebel Four" is the third story within ''Star Wars Tales'' 9, following "[[Resurrection (comic story)|Resurrection]]" and "[[Hate Leads to Lollipops]]."<ref name="Rebel Four" /> The entirety of the ninth issue was collected within the trade paperback ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 3]]'', published on [[February 26]], [[2003]].<ref name="Tales 3 date">{{DarkHorse|url=Books/12-091/Star-Wars-Tales-Volume-3-TPB|text=Star Wars Tales Volume 3 TPB}}</ref> In that collection, "The Rebel Four" was preceeded the comic story "[[Trooper (comic story)|Trooper]]," the first story in [[Star Wars Tales 10|''Star Wars Tales'' 10]].<ref name="Tales 3" /> Stephens' story was additionally republished in ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Empire Vol. 8]]'' by [[Marvel Comics]]<ref name="Epic Collection" /> on [[November 14]], [[2023]].<ref name="Random House">{{PenguinComics|url=book/?isbn=9781302953904|text=STAR WARS LEGENDS EPIC COLLECTION: THE EMPIRE VOL. 8}}</ref> "The Rebel Four" was placed between "[[Junkheap Hero]]," a 2001 story originally published in [[Star Wars Tales 6|''Star Wars Tales'' 6]], and "[[The Rebel Club]]," a story from the [[Star Wars Tales 19|nineteenth issue]] of ''Tales''.<ref name="Epic Collection" />

==Continuity==
===In-universe===
The ending of "The Rebel Four" states that Vader will face combat with a Jedi soon, referencing his duel with<ref name="Rebel Four" /> his former [[Jedi Master/Legends|master]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], depicted in the [[1977]] [[original trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']].<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref> Therefore, the events of the comic must take place during or prior to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], when the events of ''A New Hope'' occurred according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.<ref name="Date" /> The notes next to Stephens' signature at the bottom of the last page of the comic describe it as a "mash note" for 1977.<ref name="Rebel Four" /> Additionally, the ending states that Vader will eventually find peace, alluding to his redemption in the [[1983]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']].<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref>
